steps.title: Make and support the claim
steps.content: The goal is not just to say kale is a better source of vitamin C than blueberries. The response also needs evidence from the notes showing the vitamin C amounts.

steps.title: Use the two vitamin amounts
steps.content: A says both foods contain ascorbic acid, C gives the claim without evidence, and D explains why humans need vitamin C. The comparison using 120 mg for kale and 10 mg for blueberries supports the claim directly. The answer is B.
